name: Anco Chili Devil's Food Cupcakes with Roasted Macadamias
recipeIngredient: 10 tbsp Unsalted butter at room temperature
recipeInstructions:
------------------------------FOR THE TOPPING------------------------------
- Whole or chopped Macadamias (about 2 oz.)
4 oz Semisweet chocolate, chopped
1/2 cup Whipping cream
1. Preheat oven to 375°F (190°C). Butter a 9-inch square cake pan and line the bottom with parchment or waxed paper. Sift together the cake flour, cocoa, chili powder, baking soda, salt, baking powder, and cayenne.
2. In an electric mixer with a large bowl, beat the butter and sugar until light and fluffy. Beat in the eggs one at a time, continuing to beat until the mixture is light, fluffy, and nearly tripled in volume.
3. Alternate adding 1/3 of the dry mixture, half the buttermilk, and half the coffee into the butter-egg mixture. Fold in another 1/3 of the dry mixture, then the remaining buttermilk and coffee. Finish by folding in the remaining dry mixture and vanilla extract.
4. Pour the batter into the prepared pan and bake until the cake springs back when lightly touched (30-35 minutes). Remove from the pan and let cool completely on a rack.
5. Using a 2-1/2 inch cookie cutter, cut rounds from the cooled cake and place them in paper cupcake liners. Make the topping by roasting macadamias (if raw) until they begin to color slightly when roasted at 350°F (175°C).
6. Chop chocolate and heat whipping cream until it just begins to boil. Pour the hot cream over the chocolate, letting it cool to warm before drizzling over the cupcakes. Top with macadamia nuts.
[Note: Ancho chili powder can be made by steming and seeding whole ancho chilies, then pulverizing in an electric spice mill or coffee grinder. Available at markets featuring Southwestern ingredients or ordered by mail from G.B. Ratto & Co., 800-325-3483.]
